mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation expectations, as indicated by a 12-month increase in CPIAUCSL above 2%, trigger central bank tightening, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL rises > 2% YoY.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in GDP (GDPC1) and housing starts (HOUST) signals strong aggregate demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through improved corporate earnings prospects.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show no significant correlation with combined GDPC1 and HOUST growth (Pearson r < 0.1) across 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A tightening labor market, indicated by declining UNRATE below 4.5% and rising PAYEMS, creates wage pressures that reduce corporate margins, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days during late-cycle regimes.
failure_condition: SPX shows positive 20-day returns in > 50% of instances where UNRATE < 4.5% and PAYEMS > 150k.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production (INDPRO) signals weakening manufacturing activity, creating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days through reduced corporate revenue expectations.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no significant negative correlation with INDPRO declines (Pearson r > -0.15) across 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer spending, as indicated by increasing RSAFS, drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days through improved corporate sales and profit margins.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation with RSAFS increases (Pearson r < 0.1) across 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden increase in unemployment (UNRATE) above 5% signals economic distress, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days through heightened risk aversion and reduced consumer confidence.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 50% of instances where UNRATE rises > 5%.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in industrial production (INDPRO) combined with rising oil prices (not directly observable) would signal supply shocks, but since oil prices aren't in the catalog, this hypothesis uses INDPRO alone as a proxy for supply-side disruptions causing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no significant negative correlation with INDPRO declines (Pearson r > -0.15) across 3-year rolling windows.
